query_normalized: analyzing the trade‑off between prompt size and code generation quality computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (b) as it empirically measures the dependent variable (code generation/reasoning quality) in the context of prompt engineering, providing essential baseline data on how specific prompt structures (code prompting) affect model performance, which is a critical component of analyzing the prompt size vs. quality trade-off.
